steps.content: Force is defined as the product of mass and acceleration: F = mass × acceleration.
Substitute the given values: mass = 41 kg, acceleration = a m/s$^2$. So F = 41 × a = 41a.

Evaluate all choices:
  • $F = 41a$: F = 41a. This is mass (41) times acceleration (a), exactly matching F = ma. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
  • $F = 41 + a$: F = 41 + a. Addition instead of multiplication; does not represent the product of mass and acceleration. Eliminated.
  • $F = 41 - a$: F = 41 − a. Subtraction has no basis in Newton's second law. Eliminated.
  • $F = a - 41$: F = a − 41. Reversed subtraction; dimensionally inconsistent and physically meaningless. Eliminated.
